channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Measles confirmed in 3 more states as cases multiply
item.description: More than 300 cases of measles, one of the most contagious viruses in the world, have now been confirmed across more than a dozen U.S. states, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KREX 5

item.title: Judge formally sentences Adnan Syed to time served in 'Serial' podcast case
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — A Maryland judge formally sentenced Adnan Syed on Friday to the time he's already served in prison, appearing to finally bring to a close a long-running case with numerous legal twists and turns that received worldwide attention from the true-crime podcast “Serial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oz pledges to fight health care fraud but makes no commitments on Medicaid funding cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dr. Mehmet Oz promised senators on Friday to fight health care fraud and push to make Americans healthier if he becomes the next leader of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ and TOM MURPHY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Millions of people celebrate Holi, the Hindu festival of colors
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Millions of people in South Asia celebrated Holi, the Hindu festival of colors, on Friday by smearing each other with brightly colored powder, dancing to festive music and feasting on traditional sweets prepared for the occasion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Where you live can change your chances of getting dementia: Study
item.description: The study shows that air pollution and access to green spaces can affect your odds of developing dementia.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: International Delight coffee creamer shipped to 31 states is recalled
item.description: Danone, the distributor, issued a voluntary recall on some International Delight coffee creamers on Feb. 21 following reports of potential spoilage and illness related to the products' use.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, Fox 5

item.title: Voice of America ending contracts with Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France Presse
item.description: With the Trump administration moving quickly to assert its authority, the Voice of America says it has moved to cancel contracts with The Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France-Presse wire services.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Johns Hopkins University cutting more than 2,000 workers due to federal aid cuts
item.description: Johns Hopkins University said Thursday that it is cutting over 2,000 workers because of a federal funding cut.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Johns Hopkins University cutting more than 2,000 workers due to federal aid cuts
item.description: “The termination of more than $800 million in [U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID)] funding is now forcing us to wind down critical work here in Baltimore and internationally,” a spokesperson for the school said in an email.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Newborn kittens rescued from wrecked car in New York moments before demolition
item.description: A litter of kittens was rescued from the inside of a wrecked car at a junkyard moments before it was scheduled to be demolished Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KRON4

item.title: 12 taken to hospital with minor injuries after plane catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, WGN 9

item.title: Democratic Senate leader caves to Republicans in government shutdown standoff and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American Airlines Boeing 737 catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Some student loan repayment plans have been suspended: What you should know
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration's recent changes to student loans are causing frustration and confusion for some borrowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione's defense fund raises over $722K
item.description: "People are raising money not for his innocence but because they're in support of allegedly what he did.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: American Airlines plane evacuated in Denver after fire aboard
item.description: Passengers were evacuated Thursday evening from an American Airlines flight after a fire was reported.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, Fox 5

item.title: Member of '90s girl group En Vogue living in car for 3 years
item.description: A member of the once popular R&B girl group En Vogue just revealed she has been living in her car for the past three years.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: John Feinstein, one of country's foremost sports writers, dies suddenly
item.description: John Feinstein, one of the country's foremost sports writers and the author of numerous bestselling books, including the groundbreaking “A Season on the Brink” about college basketball coach Bob Knight, died unexpectedly Thursday. Feinstein was 69.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Columbine victim's death from health problems related to 1999 school shooting ruled a homicide
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The death of a woman who was partially paralyzed in the Columbine High School shooting has been ruled a homicide, raising the death toll of the 1999 attack to 14. Anne Marie Hochhalter died Feb.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Starbucks announces big changes to in-store customer experience
item.description: Starbucks is rolling out new ideas to make its stores more inviting to customers and recapture some of the "vibe" from its early days.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Las Vegas Strip hotel to be redesigned by reality TV star
item.description: Lisa Vanderpump announced Wednesday she plans to completely renovate a Strip hotel, transforming it into the "Vanderpump Hotel.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, KRON4

item.title: Trump halts doubling of tariffs on Canadian metals after Ontario suspends electricity price hikes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump 's threat Tuesday to double his planned tariffs on steel and aluminum from 25% to 50% for Canada led the provincial government of Ontario to suspend its planned surcharges on electricity sold to the United States.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ROB GILLIES and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One cat's incredible reunion with its owner after the LA wildfires
item.description: It appears the tall tale that all cats have nine lives may be true for a California Maine coon named Aggie. The beloved feline was feared dead for two months after the Palisades wildfire in Los Angeles left her family's home in ashes. But her owner, 82-year-old Katherine Kiefer, held out some hope.
item.dc:creator: BEATRICE DUPUY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas measles outbreak rises to 223 cases
item.description: The measles outbreak in the South Plains region of Texas has grown to 223 cases. 29 of those infected have been hospitalized and one school-aged child has died, according to a new update from the Texas Department of State Health Services.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stark, Fox 5

item.title: Infowars employee killed in Texas, law enforcement confirms
item.description: Jamie White, an Infowars employee, was the victim of a homicide in southeast Austin, Texas, late Sunday night, law enforcement sources confirmed to Nexstar's KXAN.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Hollis, Fox 5

item.title: Garlin Gilchrist announces Democratic bid to become Michigan's first Black governor
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Garlin Gilchrist II, a Democrat from Detroit who is Michigan's first Black lieutenant governor, announced he's jumping into the crowded 2026 race for governor on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lone Democrat to back House GOP spending bill explains decision
item.description: Rep. Jared Golden (D-Maine) defied House Democratic leadership Tuesday when he voted for the GOP’s bill to avert a government shutdown — the only one in the caucus to support the stopgap. Golden’s “yes” vote was not the decisive one, since Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: Department of Education lays off nearly half of workforce
item.description: The Department of Education announced Tuesday it was firing nearly half of its workforce, the latest Trump administration move to shrink the federal government that could face swift legal challenge.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about Mahmoud Khalil, the Columbia protester arrested by ICE and facing deportation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Palestinian activist who helped lead student protests at Columbia University faces deportation after being arrested over the weekend by federal immigration officials.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At trial's start, prosecutor blames Iran for plot to assassinate outspoken dissident
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two members of an Eastern European criminal organization were “hired guns for the government of Iran” in a plot to assassinate an Iran-born journalist at her New York City residence three years ago, a prosecutor told a federal jury in an opening statement on Tuesday at the start of a
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Blizzard Entertainment president says 'there's a game for you' no matter what type of player you are
item.description: IRVINE, Calif. (AP) — California gaming giant Blizzard Entertainment announced Thursday that its popular event BlizzCon is coming back after several years off. The celebration of all things Blizzard, which will be held at the Anaheim Convention Center, is scheduled for September 2026.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, ABC 4

item.title: Education Department says 60 universities under investigation for antisemitism
item.description: The Department of Education’s Office of Civil Rights (OCR) is investigating 60 universities for antisemitism, according to letters sent on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, ABC 4

item.title: Former US Rep. Katie Porter steps into crowded California governor's race
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former Democratic U.S. House member Katie Porter announced Tuesday that she is entering the 2026 contest for California governor, joining a crowded field of candidates that could be upended if former Vice President Kamala Harris joins the race.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 15 injured, 1 seriously, when school bus overturns in New Jersey: officials
item.description: At least 14 juveniles were injured when a school bus overturned on the Garden State Parkway in New Jersey on Monday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Social Security to again recoup 100% of overpayments
item.description: The Office of the Chief Actuary estimated the withholding rate increase could save about $7 billion over the next decade.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: PHOTOS: 132-year-old wreck of 'one of the safest ships afloat' discovered in Lake Superior
item.description: The Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society has announced the discovery of a new wreck on Lake Superior: The SS Western Reserve.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5
